requirements.description: Projects must focus on next-generation AI agents, defined as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilise tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.

AI agent design must incorporate robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow agents to approach complex tasks by breaking them down into structured subgoals. This is described in the source as a key aspect of effective AI agent design.

AI agent design must address effective memory and state management, including a balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions, to maintain coherent long-term interactions. The source describes memory and state management as necessary.
Integration of external tools and APIs is essential for overcoming limitations in large AI models and enhancing agent performance in tasks requiring accuracy and reliability.

Proposals may address one or more of the following potential research areas (not all are required per proposal): (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization capabilities; (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (c) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agents including research on mixed AI architectures; (d) advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities for real-world applications.
All proposals must incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Where possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previously funded actions.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.

Project results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities (TEFs) and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs). TEF and EDIH use is optional; contribution to the Apply AI Strategy is mandatory.

Individual proposals should position clearly within real-world Apply AI Strategy sectors. The call portfolio selection procedure ensures at least one funded project covers each of three sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving, subject to passing all evaluation thresholds. Individual proposals are not required to cover all three sectors.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
